Transcrição Completa: 4 Can I Speak to The Owner
[00:00] Okay. Um, okay. Next objection. Can I speak to the owner or better yet have a face tof face sit down with him or her?
[00:09] To be fair, right off the bat, certain instances, it is absolutely fair that they can sit down with the owner or have a face to face with him or her based on the amount of money they're putting up.
[00:21] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "High-Value Investor Scenarios"
NÚMEROS: $1M+ investments, $500K minimum for CEO meetings
VISUAL: Representação de investidores de alto valor, ícones de fundos PE, sindicatos, real estate, fundos multifamiliares, escalas monetárias crescentes
[00:23] So, as an example, we work with some some investor niches, uh, like PE firms, uh, funds, syndicates, you know, real estate guys, multif family funds, and, you know, is is sometimes very legitimate once again to have them sit down with the CEO of the organization if they're putting up like a million plus dollars.
[00:42] Um, in some institutions, you know, $500,000 might be enough money to have like a sit down with the CEO and just kind of go over it from the same perspective, but from the CEO's viewpoint.
[00:53] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Retail Demographic vs High-Value Investors"
NÚMEROS: $25,000 to $100,000 automation stores
VISUAL: Comparação visual entre investidores institucionais vs pessoas comuns, escala de valores, diferenciação de tratamento baseada em valor do investimento
[00:55] Um, in other instances, people who are putting up smaller amounts of money, like we have these automation stores that are putting up like $25,000 to $100,000, and you're typically not dealing with as serious of an investor in those instances.
[01:08] You're dealing with more of what's like a retail demographic, just regular people. And that can sometimes be a lot of money for them.
[01:15] So, to be fair, it doesn't make as much sense obviously for every person to have an opportunity to sit down, especially in a one-on-one scenario with the owner.
[01:24] However, there are certain instances where owners have the time to at least do a once a week group session with anybody who's on the fence that wants to speak with them.
[01:34] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Group Call Solution Strategy"
NÚMEROS: Thursday 6:00 PM Eastern Standard Time, once a week
VISUAL: Interface de chamada em grupo via Zoom, calendário marcando quinta-feira às 18h EST, múltiplos participantes em tela, CEO presente
[01:36] So, as an example, hey, is it possible if I can speak to the owner or like do some kind of face to face with him or her?
[01:43] Yeah, actually, every Thursday at 600 p.m. Eastern Standard Time, we have a group call with the CEO.
[01:50] Uh you're happy to come into that call, pop your camera on, he'll be there to answer any questions that you have, and you can go back and forth.
[01:58] You might even get some additional perspective from other people's questions who will also be on that call.
[02:04] And don't worry, it's not a ton of people. You'll definitely get all your questions answered.
[02:09] Um, and to be clear, there's other people who are just like you who are kind of on the fence that want to make sure they speak with that person.
[02:17] And like I said, it's a great way to be transparent and kind of learn at the same time from multiple perspectives.
[02:23] That's also a great way to overcome that objection by actually having a sitdown meeting that's over Zoom that's done once a week to make it efficient for everybody.
[02:32] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Alternative Scheduling Options"
NÚMEROS: Twice a week options, weekend and morning alternatives
VISUAL: Calendário com múltiplas opções de horários, diferentes fusos horários, flexibilidade de agendamento para acomodar diversos perfis
[02:34] Or you can do it twice a week as an example with a different time for people that might not be available at that specific time at like Thursday at 6 p.m. EST as an example.
[02:44] Maybe do it on a weekend or on some other day in the morning.
[02:48] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Direct Confrontation Strategy"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados numericamente
VISUAL: Representação de confronto direto, questões de confiança sendo endereçadas, ícones de ceticismo sendo quebrados
[02:50] Um, now to be clear, the other ways that we can overcome this objection is just typically by shutting it down altogether, but being direct about why they're asking.
[02:59] So typically it comes down to trust. Do they trust you enough as the salesperson? Probably not. It's probably why they're asking.
[03:08] you know, they might think you're full of shit or they might think that you're just not as credible, or they might think that, you know, I need this decision validated by the owner before I really choose to pull the trigger on giving you as much money as you're asking for.
[03:22] So, we want to call out that behavior.
[03:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Direct Trust Challenge Script"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados numericamente
VISUAL: Script de confrontação direta, perguntas investigativas para descobrir objeções reais, processo de quebra de resistência
[03:26] Hey, I appreciate your question. Let me just be direct with you, though. What's your intention behind asking?
[03:33] Do you not trust us? Uh, is there something that we haven't spoke about that would push you over the edge?
[03:40] Um, is there anything that we're doing that is, you know, off-putting to you or giving you skepticism about us? I'd love to address it directly.
[03:48] if you have any specific questions for me and let's get that question out of them, right?
[03:53] We want to see what that what actually is holding them back and it could play into what we talked about in that last objection handling video with using some trust and credibility assets to overcome that.
[04:04] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Group Call Recommendation"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados numericamente
VISUAL: Recomendação final destacada, eficiência para ambas as partes, CEO acessível de forma organizada
[04:06] I'd person I'd encourage doing the group call. I think the group call is a very efficient and effective way to go about getting people in direct contact with the CEO of the organization or whoever is leading the business in a very efficient way for both parties.
